seoTitle: How to Use the Zendesk Knowledge Base as an Agent
metaDescription: See how to use Zendesk's knowledge base panel to search help articles, link and quote content into replies, and check what a customer already read.
intro: Constantly retyping the same explanations slows down support agents and frustrates customers waiting for answers. Zendesk's built-in knowledge base sidebar lets agents search, preview, and drop existing help articles straight into a ticket reply without leaving the conversation. This guide shows how to search, link, quote, and even flag outdated content from inside an active ticket.

steps.heading: Open the knowledge sidebar
steps.body: While viewing an active ticket, move to the right-hand context panel and open the Knowledge Base icon to reveal the article search area.

steps.heading: Search for relevant articles
steps.body: Type keywords related to the customer's issue, or use the brand filter to narrow results to the right product line.

steps.heading: Link an article into your reply
steps.body: Hover over a matching article and use the link option to instantly attach a clean hyperlink to your response composer.

steps.heading: Quote a specific passage
steps.body: Highlight the relevant sentence in the article preview and copy the quote directly into your reply instead of paraphrasing it.

steps.heading: Check the customer's viewing history
steps.body: Review the visitor path history to see which articles the customer already read before submitting the ticket, so you don't repeat information.

steps.heading: Flag outdated content
steps.body: If an article is stale or broken, use the options menu to flag it so content managers know it needs a revision.

faq.question: Does linking an article remove my personal explanation?
faq.answer: No, you can link or quote an article alongside your own written response for extra context.

faq.question: Can I see if the customer already read the article I'm about to send?
faq.answer: Yes, the visitor path history component shows articles the customer viewed before opening the ticket.

faq.question: What happens after I flag an article as outdated?
faq.answer: Your flag notifies whoever manages the knowledge base content so they can review and update it.

faq.question: Can I filter search results by product or brand?
faq.answer: Yes, the search panel includes drop-down filters to target the right brand or language.
